title: 8 Herbal Creams For Flu
intro_desc: Herbal creams for flu can be a great alternative to traditional medicines. They work by using the properties of certain herbs to help soothe and calm the body. These creams often contain herbs like Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce fever and ease body aches. Echinacea purpurea is another herb often used in these creams, it helps boost the immune system and can reduce the severity and duration of flu symptoms. Eucalyptus globulus, also known as eucalyptus, is often added to these creams for its decongestant properties, which can help relieve congestion and make it easier to breathe. When applied topically, these herbs can provide relief from flu symptoms without the need for oral medication. This can be especially beneficial for people who are sensitive to certain medications or have trouble swallowing. Using herbal creams for flu can also be a more natural and gentle way to alleviate symptoms, reducing the risk of side effects.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains active constituents like gingerols and shogaols, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects, making them effective against flu symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger help reduce fever and alleviate body aches, while its antiviral properties inhibit the replication of the flu virus. Ginger's ability to thin mucus also makes it easier to expel, relieving congestion and coughs. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of ginger help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the flu virus.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add 10-15 grams of dried Zingiber officinale root to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix in 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil and pour the mixture into a clean container. Let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains a number of active constituents, including alkylamides, caffeic acid derivatives, and flavonoids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease congestion in the throat and sinuses, common symptoms of the flu. The flavonoids in Echinacea purpurea also have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals and support immune function. Additionally, the alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects, meaning they can help regulate the immune system's response to infection. By supporting immune function and reducing inflammation, Echinacea purpurea creams may help alleviate some of the symptoms of the flu.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers and 1 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler at 100°F (38°C) for 2 hours.
Add the dried Echinacea purpurea flowers to the coconut oil and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the coconut oil mixture. Heat until melted and pour into a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains the compound eucalyptol, which has natural decongestant properties that help relieve nasal congestion associated with flu. Eucalyptol also has anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the nose and sinuses. The creams also contain other active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the body from free radicals and reduce oxidative stress. These properties help to loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of flu symptoms. The antimicrobial properties of eucalyptol in Eucalyptus globulus creams also help combat infection and promote healing.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus essential oil, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, stirring until combined.
Remove from heat and stir in Eucalyptus globulus essential oil and vitamin E oil.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, your Eucalyptus globulus cream is ready to use. Apply as needed to relieve flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and camphor as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce the severity of flu symptoms. Rosmarinic acid specifically has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to the development of flu. Carnosic acid also exhibits antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from oxidative stress caused by the flu virus. By applying Rosmarinus officinalis creams topically, these compounds can work together to alleviate flu symptoms and promote a faster recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of rosemary essential oil, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl over a pot of boiling water.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes. Then, add rosemary essential oil and eucalyptus essential oil, and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Rosmarinus officinalis creams are ready to use for flu relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, two active constituents that help fight the flu. Thymol has antibacterial properties, which help combat secondary infections that often come with the flu. Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the body. Thymus vulgaris creams also contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help boost the immune system and reduce the severity of flu symptoms. By applying thymus vulgaris creams to the skin, the body can absorb these beneficial compounds, helping to alleviate flu symptoms and support recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of thyme essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add thyme essential oil and vitamin E oil to the melted mixture. Stir well.
Slowly add water to the mixture, stirring constantly until it thickens into a creamy consistency.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool before use. Apply to skin as needed to help soothe flu symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a key active constituent that helps alleviate flu symptoms. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the throat and nasal passages. Licorice root extracts also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects, helping to combat flu viruses. Glycyrrhizin's expectorant properties help thin and clear mucus, making it easier to breathe and cough up phlegm. By reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy immune response, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help alleviate flu symptoms, such as congestion, cough, and fever.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, and 10 drops of peppermint oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the glycerin and dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the melted oil mixture. Stir well to combine.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add the peppermint oil and stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a tin or jar and let it cool and harden completely. Once hardened, your Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra creams contains bioactive constituents like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the severity of flu symptoms. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Sambucus nigra have been shown to inhibit the replication of viruses and reduce the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. The saponins in Sambucus nigra have also been found to have immunomodulatory effects, which can help boost the immune system and fight off infections. By reducing inflammation and boosting the immune system, Sambucus nigra creams may help alleviate flu symptoms and support the body's natural recovery process.
remedies.image_alt: Sambucus nigra herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Sambucus nigra flowers and 2 cups of coconut oil in a clean glass bowl.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 30-second increments, stirring between each interval, until the oil is fully melted.
Add the dried Sambucus nigra flowers to the melted coconut oil and let it steep for 2-3 hours or overnight.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Add 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil to the strained mixture and stir until the beeswax is fully incorporated, then pour into small containers to cool and harden.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in treating flu symptoms. Linalool helps to reduce fever and relieve body aches, while linalyl acetate soothes and calms the respiratory system, easing congestion and coughs. The antiviral properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to combat the flu virus, shortening the duration of the illness. By reducing fever and inflammation, and soothing the respiratory system, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to alleviate flu symptoms and promote recovery.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for flu
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, and 1/2 cup of shea butter.
Combine the dried flowers and coconut oil in a double boiler. Heat for 2 hours, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the flowers.
Add the shea butter to the bowl and mix until combined. Let cool to room temperature.
Transfer the mixture to small jars and store in the fridge. Use as needed to relieve flu symptoms.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea might help prevent the flu, but it's not a guarantee. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can boost the immune system. They can also ease cold and flu symptoms if you do get sick. Herbal tea may be a helpful addition to a healthy lifestyle.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for flu every day is generally safe, but be cautious with the ingredients. Some teas, like eucalyptus and peppermint, can be too strong if taken regularly. Start with small amounts and listen to your body. If you feel uncomfortable or experience side effects, adjust your intake or try different teas.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for the flu can start to help within a few hours. Some people may feel relief from their symptoms like a sore throat or body aches after drinking a warm cup. As the day goes on, the effects can become more noticeable, helping to make you feel a bit more comfortable and less miserable.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for flu is best in the morning or early afternoon. At this time, your body is most receptive to natural remedies. The soothing effects of herbal tea can help ease flu symptoms and give you a boost to tackle the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for flu. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginseng, can affect blood thinners. Licorice root tea can increase blood pressure, which is a concern for people taking blood pressure medications. It's also possible for herbal teas to interact with flu medications like Tamiflu.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are usually safe, but others, like echinacea and licorice root, may stimulate the uterus or cause other issues. Always check the label for any warnings or advice for pregnant women. If you're unsure, it's better to choose a plain herbal tea or a traditional hot drink like tea or broth.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing way to feel better when you have the flu while breastfeeding. However, it's best to be cautious about what you drink. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, are generally considered safe, but others might not be. If you're unsure, you can check the ingredients and look for teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding mothers.
title_2: 8 Herbal Creams for Pregnant Women to Combat Flu
